title: Best VPN for Argentina 2026: Top Picks for Streaming, Privacy and Expats
description: Best VPN for Argentina in 2026: top picks for accessing geo-blocked content, protecting privacy, bypassing ISP throttling, and using streaming services like Netflix Argentina and Disney+.

Argentina is one of Latin America's largest internet markets, with over 35 million internet users and a well-developed streaming landscape. Whether you're a resident looking for privacy protection, an expat who wants to access content from back home, or a traveler who needs to use services unavailable in Argentina, a VPN handles all of these needs.
Why You Need a VPN in Argentina
Argentina has a free and open internet -- no government censorship of major platforms -- but there are practical reasons to use a VPN:
• ISP throttling: Some Argentine ISPs throttle streaming traffic during peak hours. A VPN encrypts your connection, preventing the ISP from identifying and throttling streaming data.
• Accessing US and European content: Services like Hulu, BBC iPlayer, and certain sports broadcasts are unavailable in Argentina. A VPN server in the US, UK, or Europe unlocks these.
• Public Wi-Fi security: Hotels, cafes, and public spaces in Buenos Aires and other cities have unsecured Wi-Fi. A VPN encrypts your connection against interception.
• Netflix Argentina library for expats: If you've moved abroad and want to keep accessing Argentine Netflix content, a VPN with Argentine servers gives you access.
• Price arbitrage: Some services offer lower prices in Argentina due to local purchasing power. A VPN with Argentine servers can let you access these prices (check terms of service).
Best VPNs for Argentina in 2026
1. NordVPN -- Best Overall

NordVPN has servers in Buenos Aires and a global network of 6000+ servers. For Argentine users accessing international content, NordVPN delivers consistently fast speeds to US and European servers. Threat Protection blocks ads and malware at the DNS level, useful on public Wi-Fi. For expats wanting Argentine content, the Buenos Aires server provides reliable access to Argentine streaming libraries. Pricing: from around $3.99/month on a 2-year plan. 30-day money-back guarantee.
2. ExpressVPN -- Best for Streaming

ExpressVPN's Lightway protocol delivers the best combination of speed and reliability for streaming. It reliably unblocks Netflix Argentina, Netflix US, Disney+, and other major platforms. Server in Argentina (Buenos Aires) plus extensive US and European server network. More expensive at around $6.67/month (annual plan), but streaming consistency justifies the cost for heavy streamers. 30-day money-back guarantee.
3. Surfshark -- Best Value

Surfshark's unlimited simultaneous connections make it ideal for households with multiple devices. At around $2.49/month (2-year plan), it's the most affordable premium option. Argentine server available. CleanWeb feature blocks ads and trackers. Streaming performance is solid, slightly less consistent than ExpressVPN for unblocking geo-restricted content. 30-day money-back guarantee.
4. CyberGhost -- Best for Beginners

CyberGhost's streaming-optimized servers are labeled by platform, making setup intuitive for first-time VPN users. Argentine servers available. Good speeds for everyday use. Less technically advanced than the top two options but easier to configure for non-technical users. From $2.03/month on a long-term plan.
Argentina-Specific Considerations

Argentina's currency situation creates some unique VPN use cases. Dollar-priced services often have different pricing when accessed from an Argentine IP address due to peso conversion policies. Before purchasing subscriptions for services like Spotify, YouTube Premium, or Apple One from an Argentine IP, verify the pricing -- you may find significant savings (or restrictions on later use from abroad).

For users in Argentina concerned about government surveillance: Argentina has had periods of economic instability and political uncertainty. While the government does not currently conduct mass internet surveillance comparable to authoritarian regimes, a no-logs VPN provides an additional layer of privacy for sensitive communications.
Free VPNs in Argentina: The Problem

Free VPNs are common but problematic. Most monetize by selling your browsing data to advertisers, which is the opposite of what privacy-conscious users want. Data caps (typically 500MB-1GB per day) make them impractical for streaming. Most don't maintain the infrastructure needed to reliably unblock Netflix and other platforms. Surfshark at $2.49/month is affordable enough that the free VPN trade-off rarely makes sense.
